Monument in front of the entrance to the Griffith Park Observatory honoring six of the world's greatest astronomers including Hipparchus (c. 190 – c. 120 BC), Nicolaus Copernicus (1473–1543), Galileo Galilei (1564–1642), Johannes Kepler (1571–1630), Isaac Newton (1642–1727), and William Herschel (1738–1822). The monument was created in 1934 by local well-known sculptors as part of the New Deal Public Works of Art Project during the Great Depression. It's unveiling took place 6 months before the Griffith Park Observatory was opened.
